titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Aaron Copland Oral History
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday October 26, 2019 at 00:00

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Aaron Copland Oral History comprises approximately 75 interviews with colleagues, friends, and family of Aaron Copland (note: for interviews with Copland himself, see the Major Figures in American Music collection). Oral History of American Music (OHAM) founder Vivian Perlis first became friends with Copland while working on the Charles Ives Oral History. Copland later wrote the preface for her book, Charles Ives Remembered. As the Ives project finished, Perlis focused on Copland as her new subject. From 1975 to 1976, she conducted many hours of interviews with Copland and those closest to him. Finally, in 1984 and 1989, their efforts culminated in the autobiographies, Copland: 1900 through 1942 and Copland: Since 1943, co-authored by Perlis.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Streaming access to sound recordings in this collection is available when connected to the Yale network, or otherwise by request.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for Oral History of American Music materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast Oral History of American Music materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: OHVI, Aaron Copland Oral History, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is restricted. Access may be granted to a patron who has obtained direct permission from the interviewee or their estate.
